problems.problem: Need audiobook narration coach for You Can Just Do Things
problems.description: The author is preparing to narrate their own audiobook for 'You Can Just Do Things' and explicitly asked a friend for recommendations for coaching. They want to exceed the normal standard and are willing to hire multiple coaches and practice for months to achieve exceptional quality.
evidence: I decided that I want to narrate my own audiobook for You Can Just Do Things. When I told a friend in the industry this and asked whether they knew any good coaches to work with
it '1 in 1000' matters to me whether the audiobook is so good no one would ever think of returning it on account of the narration
In this case, that means hiring multiple coaches and practicing for months
problems.email_open_reasoning: This is an explicit ask for help with a concrete skill need. The author literally asked for coach recommendations and states they want to hire 'multiple coaches.' They're preparing for a major project (book launch) and are willing to invest months of practice, indicating high priority and urgency.
problems.persistence_reasoning: Audiobook narration typically happens in the months leading up to publication. Given the post is from January 2026 and the author is actively seeking coaches now, this need will likely remain active for 3-4 months through preparation and recording.
problems.source_post: the lies i used to tell myself

problems.problem: Need feedback on which book excerpts resonate most with readers
problems.description: The author is publishing excerpts from their forthcoming book 'You Can Just Do Things' and explicitly asks for reader feedback to determine what content to expand or scale back in the final version. They state they're running "a few excerpts here in the next couple months, in hopes of getting feedback on the kinds of content people are excited to see in the book."
evidence: This post is an excerpt from my forthcoming book. I'll be running a few excerpts here in the next couple months, in hopes of getting feedback on the kinds of content people are excited to see in the book (which tells us what to expand or scale back).
Let me know what you think!
Sign up to be notified when my book, You Can Just Do Things, is available for purchase.
problems.email_open_reasoning: The author explicitly asks for feedback multiple times and is actively making editorial decisions based on reader input. This is a concrete, time-sensitive need during the book development process where external perspective would be valuable.
problems.persistence_reasoning: The author mentions running excerpts over "the next couple months," suggesting this is a short-term feedback gathering phase before finalizing the book. Once they've collected sufficient feedback and made editorial decisions, this need will resolve.
problems.source_post: how to instantly be better at things

problems.problem: Need feedback on book content to decide what to expand or scale back

In the post "how to increase your surface area for luck," they explicitly state they're running excerpts "in hopes of getting feedback on the kinds of content people are excited to see in the book (which is a signal about what to expand or scale back)." They need external input to shape the final manuscript.
evidence: This post is an excerpt from my forthcoming book (and builds on a couple of paragraphs in my original post on agency)

I'll be running a few excerpts here in the coming months, in hopes of getting feedback on the kinds of content people are excited to see in the book (which is a signal about what to expand or scale back)
Let me know what you think!
Sign up to be notified when my book, You Can Just Do Things, is available for purchase
problems.email_open_reasoning: The author has explicitly asked for feedback and stated this is a key decision-making factor for their book. They're actively soliciting input from readers to shape the final product, making this a clear, actionable problem where external perspectives would be valued.
problems.persistence_reasoning: Book writing projects typically take months to finalize, and the author mentions running "a few excerpts" in "coming months." The feedback collection phase appears to be ongoing through the manuscript development process, likely 3-6 months.
problems.source_post: how to increase your surface area for luck

problems.problem: Getting feedback on book content before finalizing manuscript

Let me know what you think!

problems.problem: Need publisher to recognize us as authorities on agency
problems.description: In "behold my ted talk," the author describes receiving feedback from publishers on a book proposal about agency. The nearly unanimous response was "cool idea, but who are you?" Publishers required the author and co-author Sasha to get publicly linked to the topic of agency before they would consider the book viable. The TED talk was explicitly pursued as a solution to this credibility gap.
evidence: the nearly unanimous sentiment was 'cool idea, but who are you?' Unless we could find a way to get publicly linked to the topic of agency, publishers didn't think anyone would read it.
What kind of authority on agency could I claim to be, if I couldn't even build a platform for myself?
March 13, however, was when Sasha and I got the first round of feedback from publishers on the book proposal we'd started shopping around
problems.email_open_reasoning: This was a live problem as of March 2025, and the author took action (the TED talk) to address it. However, by the post date (August 2025), the TED talk is complete and published, which was the primary credibility-building solution they pursued. The problem may be resolved or significantly mitigated, making a stranger's email about it less urgent now.
problems.persistence_reasoning: The TED talk has now been published (August 2025), which was the main solution pursued to build credibility. The problem is likely resolved or significantly reduced unless publishers still require more platform-building, but there's no indication of that in the post.

problems.problem: Pre-launch list building for 'You Can Just Do Things' book
problems.description: In 'tying yourself to the mast,' the author mentions they're currently writing a book titled 'You Can Just Do Things' and includes a signup link for pre-order notification. They note that 'for a long time that was an "it would be nice" hypothetical' but now have 'the forcing function of a book deal keeping me accountable.' This suggests they're in active book production and building an audience for launch.
evidence: Right now, I'm writing a book, and for a long time that was an 'it would be nice' hypothetical, a fantasy I considered.

In reality, writing a book contains periods of playful inspiration, but it also requires structure and discipline, which are much easier to impose when I have the forcing function of a book deal keeping me accountable.
Sign up to be notified when my book, You Can Just Do Things, is available for purchase.
problems.email_open_reasoning: While the author is actively working on a book and building a launch list, this appears to be a standard author marketing activity rather than an urgent problem or blocker. The book deal is already in place, and they're soliciting signups through their existing platform. A cold email about book marketing would be read with mild interest but isn't addressing a critical need.
problems.persistence_reasoning: Book writing with an existing deal typically spans 6-12 months from writing through publication. The author is currently in the writing phase, so launch preparation will remain relevant for at least 6 months through completion, editing, and release.
